reasoning: As of May 23, 2025, there are roughly six weeks remaining until the July 1 deadline. Discord brought on Goldman Sachs and JP Morgan in late March and appointed a new CEO in late April. Historically, the timeline from hiring bankers to filing a public S-1 spans at least 4 to 6 months, pushing a likely public filing into late summer or fall. Furthermore, reports indicate that Discord is launching new ad products "this summer" specifically to "drum up revenue potential in the lead-up to an IPO." This strongly implies that the actual IPO—and the public S-1 that precedes it by roughly 3-4 weeks—are targeted for Q4 2025 or later, allowing the new ad offerings to demonstrate impact. While they could submit a confidential Draft Registration Statement (DRS) before July 1, the resolution criteria require either a public SEC Form S-1 filing or a specific announcement on Discord's official blog. Given that companies are not obligated to announce confidential submissions—and often wait until the public S-1 flip—it is extremely unlikely Discord will meet the criteria before July 1. I am estimating a 12% probability to account for any surprises, accelerated timelines, or early blog PR.
